compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
Plan-review PASS (signed, 0 blocking, 10 advisory + 4 overflow). Triage + design decisions (all advisory; resolved here):
- [E2 fcc12 contract shape] DECIDED: kernel verification contract = Verification{index:int, severity_attributes: SeverityAttrs{prod_impact,debt_impact (none|low|medium|high), blast_radius (local|module|system), likelihood (low|medium|high), reversibility (easy|moderate|hard)}, binary: Binary{cited_reference_accurate (yes|no|insufficient|na = the VETO), + the 7 GRADED_BINARY sub-questions (yes|no|insufficient)}}. Moved verbatim from passes._pass2_model â†’ kernel; registered as 'verification' (single source); plan_review_verification registers the SAME model factory (alias).
- [E2 f812c arg shapes] DECIDED: verify_findings(findings: list[dict], *, context: str (plan text/diff), run_chunk: Callable[[instructions:str, context:str], dict], window_tokens:int, est_tokens:Callable, headroom:float). The per-gate verify-prompt PREAMBLE is the prompt file (workflow shell), not a kernel arg; context is a plain string.
- [F1 fc911 degrade assertions] DECIDED: degrade test asserts that when run_chunk raises (unparseable turn after retries), the chunk's findings get NO verification â†’ verify_findings omits their indices â†’ pass3_decide(None) â†’ INDETERMINATE (never crash). Kernel test exercises this.
- [F1 fb2c3 / E1 f6e92 golden + removal] DECIDED: parity = the existing offline workflow test (test_plan_review_workflow) asserts the verdict partition unchanged; PLUS a new kernel test asserts verify_findings reproduces the engine's merge-by-index output; PLUS a re-export-identity test (contract/listing/chunker ARE the kernel objects) proves no second copy remains.
- [E1 f5c39 veto vs 7] CLARIFIED: cited_reference_accurate is the conditional VETO (in Binary, value 'na' when no citation), separate from the 7 graded sub-questions used for validity. Documented in the kernel.
- [T4 f8731 rollback] JUSTIFIED N/A: in-process refactor, no runtime flag; rollback=git revert; the byte-equal-verdict parity test guards the merge so a regression cannot land.
- [T2 fbb48 chunking generality] ADDRESSED: kernel chunker test covers the multi-chunk boundary + merge-by-GLOBAL-index correctness (a >1-chunk case + the omitted-too-big case), validating the chunking carries over.
- [T7 f23949 / T5e fee8e docs/ADR] DEFERRED to WS4 (epic's kernel consumer-seam + doc deliverable); WS2 adds kernel module docstrings recording the verification contract + verify_findings API.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

Pass-2 (validate each finding) is the strongest reuse candidate: the binary sub-questions
(`is_verifiable`, `evidence_entails_finding`, `path_reachable`, `impact_follows_necessarily`,
`no_viable_alternative_explanation`, `no_existing_mitigation`, `severity_claim_justified` + the
cited-reference veto) and the severity-attribute enums are properties of *a finding*, not of
plans-vs-diffs â€” so a code-review finding and a plan finding answer the SAME questions. Extracting
it gives one binary vocabulary + one `verification` contract that every gate shares, eliminating
the silent-drift class. The verify orchestration already built for plan-review (token-budget
chunking, merge-by-global-index, the non-frontier verifier-model default) is likewise general and
moves with it.

## What

- Move the Pass-2 verifier into the kernel as `verify_findings(findings, *, context, model,
  headroom) -> verifications`, carrying the chunking + merge-by-index + verifier-model resolution.
- Make the `verification` pydantic/JSON contract the SINGLE registered source of the binary
  vocabulary + severity-attribute enums (one schema, small/flat).
- Reliability: validate with the existing tolerant stack (json-repair + bounded retry +
  pydantic-ai native outputs) and DEGRADE to INDETERMINATE on an unparseable turn â€” never
  hard-crash a gate. (Reuse `gate_dispatch`'s degrade path; do not add stricter validation.)
- Rewire the plan-review verify step to consume the kernel (the per-gate verify-prompt preamble +
  the plan context are passed in). No behavior change.

## Scope

In scope: the Pass-2 verify function + the `verification` contract + the tolerant-validation
reliability behavior, moved to the kernel; plan-review consumes it. Out of scope: Pass-3 (WS1, on
which this depends), the coach (WS3), the seam doc + behavioral evals (WS4). Touches
`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py` (verify builders), `sizing.py` (chunker), `workflow_ops.py`
(verify-inputs op), the verify prompt(s), and the kernel module.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The kernel exposes a `verify_findings` Pass-2 entry that takes a findings list + a domain
      context and returns the per-finding `{severity_attributes, binary}` verifications, with the
      token-budget chunking + merge-by-global-index + verifier-model default carried over.
- [ ] The `verification` contract is the single registered source of the binary-question
      vocabulary + severity-attribute enums (one schema; no second copy).
- [ ] Validation uses json-repair + bounded retry and degrades to INDETERMINATE (never crashes a
      gate) on an unparseable structured turn; a test exercises the degrade path.
- [ ] The plan-review verify step consumes the kernel; an offline (FakeRunner) test asserts the
      verifications + downstream verdict are unchanged vs before.
- [ ] `make lint` + `make check` + the full test suite pass.
compiled_state.title: WS2: Extract Pass-2 finding-verifier + the verification contract into the kernel
